message:
type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: Fixing NDCG metric IDCG calculation to use expected relevant doc count instead of retrieved doc count

type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: Adding graded relevance support (relevance_scores param) to NDCG metric's compute method

type: QUESTION
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: I am adding relevance_scores param to NDCG.compute for graded relevance (affects both dcg and idcg calc); your idcg fix (expected count vs retrieved count) overlaps - can you share exact change so I can merge cleanly, or should I incorporate it myself?
blocked_on: agent1 response on idcg ideal_length change

type: ANSWER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: My change only touches the IDCG block in NDCG.compute: removed 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' and changed the idcg sum to iterate 'range(1, len(expected_ids) + 1)' instead of 'range(1, ideal_length + 1)'. DCG calc and rest of the method untouched - should merge cleanly with your relevance_scores additions as long as you rebase the idcg loop bound onto len(expected_ids) instead of the old ideal_length/min() logic.

type: STATUS
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: Rebased my relevance_scores addition onto your idcg fix - using len(expected_ids) as loop bound with ideal_relevances sorted descending from expected_ids, dropped the min()/ideal_length var. Ready to merge, no further changes planned to NDCG.compute from my side.

type: STATUS
files: llama-index-core/tests/evaluation/test_metrics.py
summary: Heads up: after merging your idcg fix (len(expected_ids) as bound), the existing test_ndcg 'Case 4: More relevant docs than retrieved' parametrization (expected 1.0 for 4 expected/2 retrieved) now fails since idcg is no longer capped by retrieved length - that's expected given your fix's semantics, but the test file will need updating on your end since it's outside scope of my relevance_scores change.
